epic.arc: Build a work substrate where files are the environment, sessions are mortal, and nothing of value depends on any process staying alive. The log remembers; a different set of eyes countersigns every claim; an ambient loop holds the field at a workable temperature — heating stalled work, cooling runaway inflow before it floods the human. Work compounds between sessions instead of decaying.
epic.context: This is the metabolism for the wider Ontum project (the fabric of named, provenance-carrying units described in docs/phase-2/). The substrate is the harness, never the building: every capability here exists so that meaning-level work can later run on it without trusting any single agent's memory or honesty.
epic.value: You can point disposable AI sessions at real work and trust what survives, because every state is replayable from files, every claim passed a second set of eyes, and the system can tell itself to back off before it buries you.
epic.horizon: All five gates real (only L0 is today), the slow loop re-admitting its own dials from outcomes, and a corpus pointed at the machine becoming proposed atoms — the corpus-to-system move.

pieces.glue: the cell: one atom reconciled over a durable log, surviving SIGKILL with nothing lost or doubled
the field: many atoms under an admitted setpoint, with the cool path proven — the loop throttles its own inflow to protect the human stage
the first real eye: L0 judgements come from a summoned session that can say no, and the owner stamp became genuinely the owner's

the session-seam door beside the workstation fence's tool-seam wall (atom.workstation-fence.v0 / atom.workstation-write-fence.v0): a pure stdlib fold that computes a worker's own worktree from its branch and detects a claimed worker trespassing in bdo's viewport, so the prohibition never has to fire — bdo's platform-level continuation 'gateway start, platform level' (2026-06-20)

the substrate stops taxing its own waking: the part census excludes the ~82 nested worktree checkouts (~57k files) from its wiring corpus, so loop.gaps and the SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit hooks that fold through it stop stalling — and a part is no longer mis-read as wired by a clone of itself
